summary: No ship: the slice’s committed test does not actually prove the stated default-composition prompt acceptance.

findings.title: Acceptance test bypasses the default composition/runtime assembly path
findings.recommendation: Change or add the vitest to exercise the default composition assembly path, or factor/export a pure equivalent of runner assembly, then assert the assembled default prompt contains the routing marker, `## Routing policy`, the discipline heading, and no leaked placeholders. The current test reads the seed model-router prompt and seed routing config directly, so default selection, installed prompt resolution, and fallback behavior can regress while this still passes.

findings.title: Capabilities placeholder regression would still pass this gate
findings.recommendation: Assert the model-router prompt source contains both `{{routing}}` and `{{capabilities}}`, and build with a sentinel provider entry so the test proves provider `for_consumers` text reaches the assembled prompt. With `entries: []` and only `not.toContain("{{routing}}")`, a dropped `{{capabilities}}` placeholder remains invisible to this slice’s evidence.
